title: journald logs vanish on every reboot
description_md:
Post-incident reviews on this box are painful: every reboot wipes the journal, so `journalctl -b -1` (previous boot) has nothing. The team wants logs to survive reboots. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), make systemd-journald store logs persistently on disk, and confirm it's now writing there.

You're done when journald is persisting logs under `/var/log/journal`.
